businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08 and Q4 2025 gross margin of 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD is transitioning to a $99/month subscription model. Key 2026 catalysts include Cybercab production starting in April and the new Roadster unveil.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy generation and storage (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: S&P 500 avg ~23x, auto sector avg ~12x. Extreme premium persists even at -27% from 52-week high

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS $2.08. Over 4x above growth stock avg 30-40x. Robotaxi and energy growth priced in

keyMetrics.name: 2026 Delivery Guidance
keyMetrics.interpretation: Cut from 1.75M. Anti-Tesla protests and weak European sales drove the downgrade

keyMetrics.name: Q1 2026 Delivery Consensus
keyMetrics.interpretation: 23 analyst consensus. +8% YoY from Q1 2025 (336,681) but -13% vs Q4 2025 (418,227)

keyMetrics.name: WTI Oil Price
keyMetrics.interpretation: Oil broke $100 for first time since July 2022. US gas approaching $4/gal. Dual impact: boosts EV demand but pressures consumer spending

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold threshold of 30. Extreme selling pressure but technical bounce increasingly likely

growthDrivers.title: $100 Oil Era: EV Transition Catalyst
growthDrivers.description: WTI broke $100 pushing US gas toward $4/gal. CarGurus says $4 is the psychological tipping point where EV searches surge. Tesla holds 50%+ EV market share, positioning it as the primary beneficiary

growthDrivers.title: Tesla Holds 50%+ Market Share in 28% EV Sales Slump (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: While overall US EV sales dropped 28%, Tesla increased its market share above 50%. Dominant brand and charging infrastructure become stronger competitive advantages during downturns

growthDrivers.title: April Catalyst Cluster: Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 Earnings
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production launch, next-gen Roadster unveil, and Q1 earnings (4/21) all concentrated in April. Multiple catalysts within three weeks

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 Robot Video, Summer Production Start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla released Optimus3 detailed engineering footage. Targeting 1M unit production rate by end of 2026. Trefis values the robotics business at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI Chip Factory, Wedbush Calls It 'Game Changer'
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush reaffirmed $600 target citing Terafab semiconductor plans. AI5 chip pilot production in late 2026, mass production in 2027

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA Escalates FSD Investigation (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA upgraded its probe into FSD performance in fog and dust conditions. Trefis warns this could undermine the '$1 trillion AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B FSD miles data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. Competitors cannot replicate this scale of autonomous driving training data in the near term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and others now use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging all in-house. Cost advantage and faster innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and loyal fanbase generate demand without traditional marketing

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader, 100%+ growth in Europe. Price competitiveness rapidly closing the gap

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi in SF and Las Vegas. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead in autonomous ride commercialization with 200K+ paid rides per week

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Growing global EV share with Ioniq and EV6 lineups

recentNews.headline: Oil Breaks $100 as Yemen's Houthis Fire Missiles at Israel, Opening New Front
recentNews.significance: Houthi missile attack on Israel opened a new front in the Iran war. WTI rose past $100, US gas approaching $4/gal. Dual impact on Tesla: EV demand boost vs consumer spending pressure

recentNews.headline: Tesla Cuts 2026 Delivery Guidance from 1.75M to 1.69M
recentNews.significance: ARK/Cathie Wood trimmed Tesla position while TipRanks reported the delivery guidance cut. Protest impact and European sales weakness drove the reduction

recentNews.headline: Federal Regulators Open Probe Into Tesla's Camera-Only FSD System
recentNews.significance: NHTSA investigating FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Key risk to the autonomous driving valuation thesis

recentNews.headline: Millions Join 'No Kings' Protests, 200+ Events Target Tesla
recentNews.significance: Global protests with millions of participants. Tesla directly targeted due to Musk's DOGE political involvement. Over 200 anti-Tesla protests

recentNews.headline: Tesla Emerges as Big Winner in Brutal 28% EV Sales Slump
recentNews.significance: Tesla increased market share above 50% even as the overall EV market dropped 28%. Proves competitive strength during downturns

analystOpinions.notableComment: HSBC $119 vs Stifel $508, Wedbush $600. Extreme polarization persists. Oil at $100 worsens macro but also accelerates EV adoption

risks.title: Oil Breaks $100, Houthi Entry Escalates War
risks.description: WTI crossed $100 as Houthis fired missiles at Israel. US gas approaching $4/gal could boost EV demand but also crushes consumer spending and reignites inflation fears, pressuring all high-multiple tech

risks.title: Delivery Guidance Cut to 1.69M, Growth Slowdown Confirmed
risks.description: Cut from 1.75M due to protest impact and European weakness. Even ARK/Cathie Wood reduced positions, shaking the most prominent bull case

risks.title: Below Both 50-Day and 200-Day Moving Averages, RSI 33 Oversold
risks.description: Trading below SMA50 ($387) and SMA200 ($400). RSI at 33 near oversold. Monday oil surge and Houthi escalation add further downside pressure

risks.title: HSBC $119 Target, ARK Selling Simultaneously
risks.description: Both the most prominent bear (HSBC) and bull (ARK) showing negative signals. Unprecedented alignment

risks.title: FSD Regulatory Risk Intensifying
risks.description: NHTSA probe escalated to camera-only system investigation. Trefis warns of threat to the '$1 trillion AI thesis'

risks.title: Extreme Valuation Risk
risks.description: TTM P/E 335x is 14.5x the S&P 500 average. Delivery guidance cut makes valuation justification even harder

buyReasons.title: $100 Oil Makes Tesla the Top EV Transition Beneficiary
buyReasons.rationale: US gas at $4/gal triggers EV search surge per CarGurus. With 50%+ EV market share, Tesla captures the lion's share of accelerated EV demand

buyReasons.title: April Catalyst Concentration
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, Netherlands FSD approval, and Q1 earnings (4/21) all in April

buyReasons.title: RSI 33, Technical Oversold Territory
buyReasons.rationale: RSI near the 30 oversold threshold. -27% from 52-week high. Technical bounce probability increasing

buyReasons.title: SpaceX IPO + Optimus Robot, Long-Term Revaluation Catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: SpaceX IPO rumors and Optimus3 production start could justify long-term valuation premium

overallOpinion: Houthis fired missiles at Israel, expanding the Iran war. Oil broke WTI $100 with US gas approaching $4/gal. This accelerates EV transition demand but simultaneously pressures consumer spending and fuels inflation fears across tech
Tesla cut delivery guidance from 1.75M to 1.69M. ARK sold. Protests are now hitting actual numbers. HSBC $119 and Stifel $508 targets show a 5x gap reflecting extreme market division
April catalysts are concentrated within three weeks: Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings. EV market share held above 50% in a 28% slump, and $100 oil could turbocharge EV adoption
$361.83, RSI 33 oversold. Below SMA50 ($387) and SMA200 ($400). Oil surge and Houthi escalation will add Monday volatility. Hold, wait for 4/21 earnings confirmation

investmentVerdict.summary: Oil at $100 and Houthi entry raise macro risk a notch. EV demand acceleration is a positive, but delivery guidance cuts and spending concerns coexist. RSI 33 and April catalysts provide bounce potential, but war escalation increases uncertainty. Q1 earnings on 4/21 becomes even more critical

sources.description: Houthi missile attack on Israel, WTI breaks $100

sources.name: Forbes - Iran Conflict, $100 Oil Impact
sources.description: WTI from $67 to $100, consumer and economic impact analysis

sources.description: Oil's record monthly surge driven by Iran war

sources.description: Tesla 2026 delivery outlook cut to 1.69M, ARK $84M tech sell-off

sources.name: Simply Wall St - FSD Probe
sources.description: Federal regulators open camera-only FSD investigation

sources.name: InsideEVs - Tesla Wins in EV Slump
sources.description: Tesla market share rises above 50% in 28% EV sales decline

sources.name: Meyka - Oil Rise and Tesla EV Demand
sources.description: High oil lifts EV interest but also pressures household budgets

witty.hook: Oil at $100 and you're still driving gas? Tesla's best ad campaign just wrote itself
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83(-2.76%)

Oil $100. Houthis join the war.
Gas at $4/gal. EV searches exploding.

But Tesla stock is down.
Because stock markets and logic
don't always share a car.

1/4
thread:
Guidance cut: 1.75M to 1.69M.
ARK sold. Protests hit real numbers.

People have reasons to buy Teslas
and reasons not to. At the same time.

2/4

EV share 50%+ in a market down 28%.
$100 oil makes that number go higher.

The question is whether people
can afford to buy anything at all.

3/4

RSI 33. Oversold. April is packed.
But war could bury the catalysts.
Hold.

4/4

empathy.hook: TSLA holders, oil at $100, war expanding, stock dropping. Hang in there

Tesla captures 50%+ of that demand.

3/4
